    "R1.1",

    "RX.5"
  ],
  "category_focus": "R1",
  "clarification_needed": true,
  "clarification_question": "Which focus should I prioritize for this section: A) language polish, B) structure/flow, C) evidence–claim alignment, D) novelty/overclaim checks, or E) citation/attribution? You may pick one primary (and optionally a secondary).",
  "rationale": {
    "R1.1": "The section culminates in a contribution claim that may benefit from explicit reframing of the thesis and gap at the section level.",
    "R1.2": "The argument links setup and ablations to a general claim, so validating the logical chain from premises to conclusions is important.",
    "R1.3": "The performance and ablation claims must match the reported numbers and experimental descriptions in the text.",
    "R1.4": "The 'first demonstration' positioning requires estimating delta versus nearest prior structural detectors named in the section.",
    "R1.6": "Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and register can be improved without altering higher-level structure or content.",
    "RX.1": "Any guidance should highlight where uncertainty or limitations ought to be acknowledged given the presented evidence.",
    "RX.2": "The wording should avoid unqualified or absolute claims that exceed what the reported results support.",
    "RX.3": "Assistance will remain scoped to this section and the specified responsibilities, not altering experiments or adding new content.",
    "RX.4": "Recommendations should avoid encouraging misleading reporting or benchmark gaming that could harm research integrity.",
    "RX.5": "All judgments should be explicitly tied to the content and numbers present in the provided text for traceability."
  }
}

parsed.clarification_question: Which focus should I prioritize for this section: A) language polish, B) structure/flow, C) evidence–claim alignment, D) novelty/overclaim checks, or E) citation/attribution? You may pick one primary (and optionally a secondary).

rationale.R1.1: The section culminates in a contribution claim that may benefit from explicit reframing of the thesis and gap at the section level.
rationale.R1.2: The argument links setup and ablations to a general claim, so validating the logical chain from premises to conclusions is important.
rationale.R1.3: The performance and ablation claims must match the reported numbers and experimental descriptions in the text.
rationale.R1.4: The 'first demonstration' positioning requires estimating delta versus nearest prior structural detectors named in the section.
rationale.R1.6: Sentence- and paragraph-level clarity and register can be improved without altering higher-level structure or content.
rationale.RX.1: Any guidance should highlight where uncertainty or limitations ought to be acknowledged given the presented evidence.
rationale.RX.2: The wording should avoid unqualified or absolute claims that exceed what the reported results support.
rationale.RX.3: Assistance will remain scoped to this section and the specified responsibilities, not altering experiments or adding new content.
rationale.RX.4: Recommendations should avoid encouraging misleading reporting or benchmark gaming that could harm research integrity.
rationale.RX.5: All judgments should be explicitly tied to the content and numbers present in the provided text for traceability.
